key_insight: The paper adds Lagrange multipliers to evolving surface FEM for mean curvature flow so the discrete method preserves the energy-decreasing/area-decreasing structure while remaining high-order accurate.
engineering_relevance: This gives computational mechanics and geometric simulation users a stable, structure-preserving way to evolve closed surfaces with provable error bounds and low overhead.
method: Uses a solution-driven formulation based on Huisken’s equations for the normal vector and mean curvature.
Applies linearly implicit BDF time stepping for geometric variables and implicit Adams updates for nodal positions.
Allows minimal-deformation-rate tangential motion while enforcing area decrease via a discrete Lagrange multiplier.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports optimal-order H1 error bounds of O(h^k+τ^q) for k≥2 and q=2…5, plus sphere simulations confirming the predicted convergence rates and showing only small overhead.
watch_item: The guarantees rely on sufficient regularity and careful nonlinear multiplier solves, so robustness on highly irregular meshes or singular surface evolution may need extra verification.
paper_title: Structure-preserving Lagrange-multiplier methods for mean curvature flow and their error bounds
